# UC-13.4.15 · Splunk MLTK and DSDL Model Drift, Performance Decay, and Retraining Trigger Detection (PSI and KS input drift, prediction and calibration drift, concept drift via accuracy regression, label lag, staleness, shadow gaps, retrain failures, cardinality and feature-store schema drift)

> **Criticality:** Critical &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Reliability, Performance &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We watch the kitchen that turns everyday signals into predictions, like a chef whose suppliers quietly swap grains without updating the recipe card. When the mix shifts, the soup still simmers, but the flavor wanders and guests stop coming back. We raise a clear flag when the blend drifts so the recipe gets retested before anyone serves a bad bowl.*

---

## Description

Detects Splunk-native model lifecycle risk across Machine Learning Toolkit ML-SPL models and Data Science Deep Learning container endpoints by fusing PSI and KS proxy metrics, rolling accuracy decay against baselines, apply latency tails, staleness from last fit times, repeated retrain failures, delayed label feedback, sustained shadow-versus-active performance gaps, cardinality stress signals, and feature-store join breakage context enriched with inventory ownership.

## Value

Model risk owners and platform leaders receive an explainable, multi-lane narrative that separates true distribution shift and concept drift from pipeline lag and inventory gaps before champion models silently harm revenue, safety, or compliance outcomes, while engineers gain routing tokens that point to reproducible retrain workflows, Python pins, and Kubernetes capacity decisions rather than ad hoc guesswork.

## Implementation

Land MLTK apply metrics into mltk_model_metrics.csv or mltk:apply:metrics, REST snapshots of persisted models, _internal mlspl.log diagnostics, DSDL dsdl:metrics from Kubernetes inference, and ml_feedback label gaps; maintain mltk_model_inventory.csv and cmdb ml_model_inventory; normalize model_name, env, alg; save platform_uc_13_4_15_mltk_dsdl_drift_retrain every fifteen minutes with earliest=-24h@h; route critical and high severities to ML platform on-call; archive weekly evidence exports with model risk tags.

## Evidence

Saved search platform_uc_13_4_15_mltk_dsdl_drift_retrain on a fifteen-minute schedule; dashboard drilldowns across MLTK metrics, REST snapshots, mlspl diagnostics, DSDL metrics, and feedback lanes; alert routing to ML platform on-call; weekly CSV exports archived to a restricted model risk index.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

In a lab model, ingest mltk_model_metrics.csv rows with psi_score above 0.26 and dsdl:metrics rows showing elevated inference_p95_ms while mlspl.log records repeated failures, confirm platform_uc_13_4_15_mltk_dsdl_drift_retrain returns critical or high with non-null recommended_action within two fifteen-minute windows.

### Negative scenario

After recovery when baselines refresh, mlspl errors subside, labels arrive within agreed lag, and shadow gaps normalize, confirm severities return to low or medium noise bands and the alert wrapper emits zero rows when filtering to critical and high only.

## Detailed Implementation

### Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with ML platform engineering, data science governance, search and KV store operators, Kubernetes platform teams, and observability engineers who land container sidecar metrics from Deep Learning Toolkit scoring paths. This use case is deliberately not UC-13.4.1 managed large-language-model HTTP latency fusion, not UC-13.4.2 token cost burn-rate economics, and not UC-13.4.3 accelerator duty-cycle telemetry. Here the evidence story is Splunk-native model lifecycle integrity for Machine Learning Toolkit ML-SPL artifacts and Data Science Deep Learning container endpoints: whether input feature distributions drift relative to training baselines, whether posterior predictions shift without ground-truth confirmation, whether concept drift erodes accuracy and F1 after policy or market regime changes, whether probability calibrations lose reliability, whether label feedback arrives too late to close learning loops, whether models exceed acceptable staleness windows, whether shadow challengers beat production for sustained intervals without promotion governance, whether automated retrain jobs fail repeatedly, whether training datasets explode in cardinality after schema or catalog changes, and whether feature-store contracts break joins that silently zero out important attributes.

Machine Learning Toolkit brings fit, apply, score, summary, and partial_fit patterns into the search language so practitioners can train DensityFunction estimators, RandomForestClassifier ensembles, state-space forecasts, and related algorithms with artifacts persisted under the Splunk management surface. Those models often live in collections backed by KV store or bundle-attached model directories depending on deployment choices; operators must know which storage class holds each artifact because backup, replication, and access-control stories differ. The Python for Scientific Computing add-on supplies the CPython stack and pinned scientific packages that deterministic golden-container builds expect; drift in those pins across search heads can change numerical results even when training SPL is unchanged. Deep Learning Toolkit extends the same operational envelope with container images that host TensorFlow, PyTorch, or custom scoring code, frequently scheduled on Kubernetes workers with GPU or CPU classes, emitting inference latency, batch shapes, GPU memory pressure, and custom drift metrics from sidecars into dedicated indexes.

Population Stability Index and two-sample Kolmogorov-Smirnov statistics are common distribution-shift gauges for top features and scored outputs. PSI buckets compare expected versus observed proportions; practitioners choose stable bin edges from training quantiles and refresh those edges only under controlled versioning. KS statistics complement PSI by highlighting shape changes that bucketization might mask, especially when tails move. Neither test replaces causal investigation; both prioritize which features deserve human review before retrain spend. Prediction drift tracks shifts in the model output margin even when labels are sparse, while concept drift detection leans on delayed labels, analyst adjudications, or automated outcomes joined back to prediction identifiers.

RBAC is mandatory. Service accounts that call fit and apply in production must not share interactive analyst credentials. REST pollers that snapshot model metadata need read-only capabilities on the search head or dedicated management node. Container registries that store Deep Learning Toolkit images require immutable tags and signed manifests; GitOps repositories should record the Dockerfile revision, dependency lockfiles, and Splunk app version triple that produced each image. Evidence retention must satisfy internal model risk policy and external frameworks without storing prohibited sensitive attributes in drift logs; hash or tokenize customer identifiers before indexing when legal requires minimization.

Index design lands complementary sourcetypes without forcing one parser to pretend it understands every lane. Typical patterns include a curated metrics index carrying mltk apply metrics or summarized CSV snapshots from scheduled experiments, management REST snapshots materialized into metrics indexes for model titles and algorithm names, _internal splunkd lines from mlspl.log narrating fit and apply failures plus execution latency, dsdl metrics lines from inference services with container ids and framework tags, and feedback label lines with ground-truth timestamps for join latency analytics. Stamp change_ticket_id on maintenance that touches feature pipelines, lookup tables, or container image tags so validators correlate benign shifts.

Field normalization expectations: model_name must be a stable string shared across ML-SPL models, REST titles, container environment variables, and inventory lookups; avoid human-readable display names that change weekly. env should separate production, staging, and sandbox scoring paths so shadow models do not pollute production baselines. alg captures RandomForestClassifier, DensityFunction, tf_savedmodel, torch_script, or similar tokens for routing runbooks. psi_score and ks_stat should arrive as floats already computed by feature monitoring jobs when possible; this detection tolerates nulls by coalescing to zero only after explicit gating flags show monitoring silence versus true zeros. accuracy_7d represents recent rolling accuracy or proxy F1 for the business outcome; accuracy_baseline and accuracy_drift_pct should be computed relative to a frozen champion snapshot or rolling long-window median per model. prediction_p95_ms should reflect end-to-end scoring latency including serialization and KV retrieval. last_trained_age_days must derive from authoritative fit timestamps, not from informal wiki notes. retrain_failure_count should increment only on workflow failures after retries exhaust. label_lag_days measures delay between prediction time and label availability for the same primary key.

Risk briefing: silent feature-store join breakage can present as sudden PSI stability because missing values collapse variance while accuracy collapses elsewhere; combine schema contract checks with drift math. Class imbalance flips after policy changes can improve naive accuracy while harming minority-class recall; keep F1 and calibration metrics alongside accuracy. Shadow models that outperform production for two weeks may indicate promotion opportunity or a leakage bug; require independent validation before traffic shifts. Scheduled-search backlog can delay apply steps and mimic model staleness when predictions are simply late; separate pipeline lag from parameter drift.

Differentiation recap: Splunk-native MLTK and DSDL model lifecycle drift, calibration, staleness, shadow gaps, retrain failures, cardinality explosions, and feature-store schema drift, not external LLM provider HTTP analytics.

FinOps and reliability linkage: tie recommended_action tokens to runbooks that reference Python environment pins, dataset manifests, and Git commit hashes for reproducibility.

SLO companion: mirror summarized tuples into a summary index keyed by model_name and env for quarterly model risk committee reviews when executives ask whether scoring remains trustworthy after upstream data changes.

Closing prerequisite checklist: confirm Machine Learning Toolkit and Deep Learning Toolkit versions, Python for Scientific Computing build, Kubernetes node pools for containers, KV store health for model collections, and inventory lookups maintained by data platform stewards.

### Step 2 — Configure data collection

Stand up five complementary lanes the multisearch fans wide, plus CSV governance artifacts that keep ownership, cadence, and accuracy service levels out of SPL literals.

Lane A, curated MLTK metrics snapshots: schedule a short search that writes daily or hourly drift metrics into mltk_model_metrics.csv in the Splunk lookup directory, or land sourcetype lines such as mltk:apply:metrics with psi_top_feature, ks_top_feature, rolling accuracy, champion baseline, apply latency percentiles, last fit epoch, retrain failure counters, and shadow minus active accuracy gaps. Ensure the job runs on a search head that can read the same KV collections production scoring uses, or you will compare drift evidence against the wrong artifact generation.

Lane B, model inventory REST snapshots: optionally schedule a lightweight poll of the data models endpoint that records title, app, updated time, and owning namespace fields into a metrics index for staleness math. Where REST is forbidden in scheduled production jobs, materialize the same snapshot with an external orchestrator and ingest JSON with a dedicated sourcetype. Keep credentials in the password storage facility and rotate on the same cadence as other platform secrets.

Lane C, mlspl execution diagnostics: forward or search locally index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd from sources matching mlspl.log on hosts that execute fit and apply. Capture errors referencing memory limits, incompatible algorithm options, partial_fit failures, and version skew after upgrades. Correlate timestamps with change records for Python for Scientific Computing and Machine Learning Toolkit app installs.

Lane D, DSDL container inference metrics: land dsdl:metrics events from Kubernetes pods running TensorFlow or PyTorch servers into index=dsdl_inference. Include inference_p95_ms, prediction_psi, prediction_ks, model_age_days, train_job_fail_count, and shadow_gap_acc emitted by sidecars or the inference proxy. Map k8s_namespace to env for consistent bucketing with MLTK lanes.

Lane E, label feedback gap analytics: land feedback:label events into index=ml_feedback with model_name, predictor_id, label_gap_days, and authoritative arrival timestamps. This lane powers SLA analytics for ground-truth latency even when accuracy metrics look stable because labels arrived early only for a biased subset.

Lookup maintenance: publish mltk_model_inventory.csv with model_name, owner_team, business_use_case, retrain_cadence_days, sla_accuracy, and runbook_url. Refresh after reorganizations. Maintain cmdb sourcetype=ml_model_inventory with supplemental owner and criticality fields for left joins when CSV rows go stale.

props.conf guidance: set TRUNCATE safely for wide JSON from REST or sidecars, honor TIME_PREFIX from container and Splunk timestamps, and FIELDALIAS alternate field names for psi and ks metrics when data science pipelines rename columns between releases. transforms.conf can hash sensitive customer keys while preserving join identifiers.

Pre-save validation: confirm each lane returns rows in the trailing window during business traffic, that a synthetic PSI spike in lab crosses thresholds, and that inventory joins resolve owner_team without null spikes that masquerade as unowned models.

Example operator helpers for design reviews from hardened bastions:

Use the search interface to validate that mltk_model_metrics.csv exists and returns non-zero row counts for champion models. Use kubectl logs on a DSDL inference deployment to confirm sidecar exporters emit prediction histogram summaries. Use your GitOps history to prove image tags match the runbook narrative pinned for each model_name.

### Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as platform_uc_13_4_15_mltk_dsdl_drift_retrain with a fifteen minute schedule during steady production, dispatch.earliest_time=-24h@h, dispatch.latest_time=now, and alert when severity is critical or high after deduplication. Throttle duplicate model_name and env tuples for thirty minutes unless severity escalates. Include recommended_action, lanes, and calibration hints in pager bodies so responders open feature-store contracts, container registries, and retrain workflows without guesswork.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch merges lookup-backed MLTK metrics, REST model staleness hints, _internal mlspl diagnostics, DSDL container drift metrics, and label feedback gap summaries so a silent single lane cannot hide a genuine lifecycle breach. bucket span=1h aligns series before stats chooses max drift signals and perc95 latency per tuple. streamstats window=168 approximates a seven-day rolling average of accuracy per model_name and env when sparse hourly buckets would otherwise flicker. eventstats median and max baselines stabilize accuracy_drift_pct denominators without quarterly spreadsheet edits. case maps combined PSI, KS proxy, accuracy drift percent, staleness days, retrain failure counts, label lag, shadow gaps, and latency pressure into severity tiers. join enriches mltk_model_inventory.csv and cmdb ml_model_inventory for owner_team and business_use_case. coalesce ladders absorb field renames across MLTK and DSDL frameworks.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.4.15 MLTK and DSDL model drift performance decay retrain trigger fusion mltk_model_metrics REST models mlspl dsdl sidecar feedback labels earliest=-24h@h")`
| multisearch
    [ | inputlookup mltk_model_metrics.csv
      | eval lane="mltk_lookup_metrics"
      | eval model_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model_name, model_id, "unknown_model"))))
      | eval env=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(env, environment, scoring_env, "prod"))))
      | eval alg=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(alg, algorithm, "mltk_ml_spl"))))
      | eval psi_score=tonumber(coalesce(psi_top_feature, psi_score, 0))
      | eval ks_stat=tonumber(coalesce(ks_top_feature, ks_stat, 0))
      | eval accuracy_7d=tonumber(coalesce(accuracy_rolling_7d, accuracy_7d, 0))
      | eval accuracy_baseline=tonumber(coalesce(accuracy_champion_baseline, accuracy_baseline, 0))
      | eval prediction_p95_ms=tonumber(coalesce(apply_latency_p95_ms, prediction_p95_ms, 0))
      | eval last_trained_epoch=tonumber(coalesce(last_fit_epoch, 0))
      | eval last_trained_age_days=if(last_trained_epoch>0, round((now()-last_trained_epoch)/86400,2), tonumber(coalesce(last_trained_age_days, 0)))
      | eval retrain_failure_count=tonumber(coalesce(retrain_fail_7d, retrain_failure_count, 0))
      | eval label_lag_days=tonumber(coalesce(label_feedback_lag_days, label_lag_days, 0))
      | eval shadow_acc=tonumber(coalesce(shadow_accuracy_7d, 0))
      | eval active_acc=tonumber(coalesce(active_accuracy_7d, accuracy_7d, 0))
      | eval shadow_minus_active_acc=round(shadow_acc-active_acc,4)
      | fields _time lane model_name env alg psi_score ks_stat accuracy_7d accuracy_baseline prediction_p95_ms last_trained_age_days retrain_failure_count label_lag_days shadow_minus_active_acc ]
    [ | rest /services/data/models splunk_server=local count=0
      | eval lane="rest_data_models"
      | eval model_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(title, "unknown_model"))))
      | eval env=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(app, "prod"))))
      | eval alg="mltk_persisted_model"
      | eval psi_score=0
      | eval ks_stat=0
      | eval accuracy_7d=0
      | eval accuracy_baseline=0
      | eval prediction_p95_ms=0
      | eval last_trained_age_days=if(isnotnull(updated) AND len(toString(updated))>=10, round((now()-strptime(substr(toString(updated),1,19), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"))/86400,2), 0)
      | eval retrain_failure_count=0
      | eval label_lag_days=0
      | eval shadow_minus_active_acc=0
      | fields _time lane model_name env alg psi_score ks_stat accuracy_7d accuracy_baseline prediction_p95_ms last_trained_age_days retrain_failure_count label_lag_days shadow_minus_active_acc ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd source=*mlspl.log earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval lane="internal_mlspl_apply_fit"
      | rex field=_raw max_match=1 "(?i)model[^=]*=\s*(?<ml_name>[A-Za-z0-9_.-]+)"
      | eval model_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(ml_name, "unknown_model"))))
      | eval env="prod"
      | eval alg="mltk_ml_spl"
      | eval psi_score=0
      | eval ks_stat=0
      | eval accuracy_7d=0
      | eval accuracy_baseline=0
      | eval prediction_p95_ms=tonumber(coalesce(apply_duration_ms, exec_duration_ms, 0))
      | eval last_trained_age_days=0
      | eval retrain_failure_count=if(match(lower(_raw),"error|exception|failed|oom|killed"),1,0)
      | eval label_lag_days=0
      | eval shadow_minus_active_acc=0
      | bucket _time span=15m
      | stats max(_time) AS _time perc95(prediction_p95_ms) AS prediction_p95_ms sum(retrain_failure_count) AS retrain_failure_count BY model_name env alg lane ]
    [ search index=dsdl_inference sourcetype=dsdl:metrics earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval lane="dsdl_container_metrics"
      | eval model_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(dsdl_model, model_name, container_model, "unknown_model"))))
      | eval env=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_namespace, env, "prod"))))
      | eval alg=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(framework, "pytorch_tf"))))
      | eval psi_score=tonumber(coalesce(prediction_psi, feature_psi, 0))
      | eval ks_stat=tonumber(coalesce(prediction_ks, 0))
      | eval accuracy_7d=tonumber(coalesce(batch_accuracy_7d, 0))
      | eval accuracy_baseline=tonumber(coalesce(batch_accuracy_baseline, 0))
      | eval prediction_p95_ms=tonumber(coalesce(inference_p95_ms, scoring_latency_ms, 0))
      | eval last_trained_age_days=tonumber(coalesce(model_age_days, 0))
      | eval retrain_failure_count=tonumber(coalesce(train_job_fail_count, 0))
      | eval label_lag_days=tonumber(coalesce(label_delay_days, 0))
      | eval shadow_minus_active_acc=tonumber(coalesce(shadow_gap_acc, 0))
      | fields _time lane model_name env alg psi_score ks_stat accuracy_7d accuracy_baseline prediction_p95_ms last_trained_age_days retrain_failure_count label_lag_days shadow_minus_active_acc ]
    [ search index=ml_feedback sourcetype=feedback:label earliest=-30d@d latest=now
      | eval lane="label_feedback_gap"
      | eval model_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model_name, predictor_id, "unknown_model"))))
      | eval env=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(env, "prod"))))
      | eval alg="labeled_feedback"
      | eval psi_score=0
      | eval ks_stat=0
      | eval accuracy_7d=0
      | eval accuracy_baseline=0
      | eval prediction_p95_ms=0
      | eval last_trained_age_days=0
      | eval retrain_failure_count=0
      | eval label_lag_days=tonumber(coalesce(label_gap_days, feedback_lag_days, 0))
      | eval shadow_minus_active_acc=0
      | stats max(_time) AS _time max(label_lag_days) AS label_lag_days BY model_name env alg lane ]
| eval model_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model_name, "unknown_model"))))
| eval env=coalesce(env, "prod")
| eval alg=coalesce(alg, "unknown_alg")
| bucket _time span=1h
| stats values(lane) AS lanes max(psi_score) AS psi_score max(ks_stat) AS ks_stat max(accuracy_7d) AS accuracy_7d max(accuracy_baseline) AS accuracy_baseline perc95(prediction_p95_ms) AS prediction_p95_ms max(last_trained_age_days) AS last_trained_age_days sum(retrain_failure_count) AS retrain_failure_count max(label_lag_days) AS label_lag_days max(shadow_minus_active_acc) AS shadow_minus_active_acc BY _time model_name env alg
| sort model_name env + _time
| streamstats window=168 global=f current=t avg(accuracy_7d) AS accuracy_7d_roll BY model_name env
| eval accuracy_7d=if(accuracy_7d>0, accuracy_7d, accuracy_7d_roll)
| eventstats median(accuracy_7d) AS accuracy_med_global BY model_name
| eval accuracy_baseline=if(accuracy_baseline>0, accuracy_baseline, coalesce(accuracy_med_global, accuracy_7d, 0))
| eval accuracy_drift_pct=if(accuracy_baseline>0.0001, round(100*(accuracy_baseline-accuracy_7d)/accuracy_baseline,2), 0)
| eventstats max(accuracy_baseline) AS acc_base_max BY model_name
| eval accuracy_baseline=coalesce(acc_base_max, accuracy_baseline)
| join type=left max=0 model_name
    [| inputlookup mltk_model_inventory.csv
     | eval model_name=lower(trim(toString(model_name)))
     | fields model_name owner_team business_use_case retrain_cadence_days sla_accuracy runbook_url ]
| join type=left max=0 model_name
    [| search index=cmdb sourcetype=ml_model_inventory earliest=-7d@h latest=now
     | dedup model_name
     | eval model_name=lower(trim(toString(model_name)))
     | fields model_name cmdb_owner_team cmdb_criticality]
| eval owner_team=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(owner_team, cmdb_owner_team, "unowned"))))
| eval business_use_case=coalesce(business_use_case, "unspecified_outcome")
| eval severity=case(psi_score>0.25 OR accuracy_drift_pct>10 OR last_trained_age_days>90 OR retrain_failure_count>=2, "critical", psi_score>0.20 OR accuracy_drift_pct>7 OR last_trained_age_days>60 OR label_lag_days>7 OR shadow_minus_active_acc>0.05, "high", psi_score>0.10 OR accuracy_drift_pct>4 OR prediction_p95_ms>5000 OR retrain_failure_count>=1, "medium", true(), "low")
| eval recommended_action=case(severity=="critical" AND psi_score>0.25, "freeze_scoring_rebucket_psi_validate_feature_contracts_and_schema", severity=="critical" AND accuracy_drift_pct>10, "rollback_model_promotion_retrain_with_fresh_labels_and_regime_features", severity=="critical" AND last_trained_age_days>90, "schedule_forced_retrain_verify_python_pips_and_mltk_dsdl_versions", severity=="critical" AND retrain_failure_count>=2, "repair_retrain_pipeline_check_memory_limits_and_dataset_cardinality", severity=="high" AND label_lag_days>7, "fix_label_feedback_ingest_tighten_ground_truth_join_sla", severity=="high" AND shadow_minus_active_acc>0.05, "open_shadow_promotion_review_independent_leakage_audit", severity=="high", "deep_dive_feature_store_joins_and_calibration_curves", severity=="medium", "tighten_monitoring_buckets_refresh_baseline_snapshot", true(), "continue_sampling_drift_metrics_and_champion_challenger_evidence")
| table _time model_name env alg owner_team business_use_case psi_score ks_stat accuracy_7d accuracy_drift_pct prediction_p95_ms last_trained_age_days retrain_failure_count label_lag_days severity recommended_action
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

[platform_uc_13_4_15_mltk_dsdl_drift_retrain_alert]
cron_schedule = */15 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -24h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
action.email = 1
action.email.to = platform-ml-ops-oncall@example.com
action.email.subject = ML drift $result.severity$ model=$result.model_name$ env=$result.env$
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch platform_uc_13_4_15_mltk_dsdl_drift_retrain | where severity IN ("critical","high")

Performance: if Job Inspector shows multisearch queue time above objectives, materialize per-arm summaries hourly into summary indexes keyed on model_name and env, widen alert windows on summaries, and retain this full search for investigations.

### Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins in model governance systems: compare champion-challenger dashboards, offline evaluation notebooks, and feature-store data contracts against Splunk rows for the same model_name and hour bucket. When Splunk lags, verify lookup refresh jobs, HEC token health for container exporters, and Kubernetes log shipping backpressure before muting detections.

Deep dive when PSI is calm but accuracy collapses: inspect null rates after joins, verify training-serving skew, and replay a sample batch through the current apply pipeline to catch silent defaults.

Calibration drift validation: review reliability diagrams and expected calibration error metrics side by side with this detection output; a model can show stable accuracy while probability rankings become unusable for threshold policies.

Synthetic drift: inject a controlled shift in a non-production feature distribution, confirm psi_score and ks_stat arms respond within two windows, then restore baselines and verify decay.

Negative test: after recovery when REST shows fresh updated times, mlspl errors return to baseline, DSDL latency is within budget, and labels arrive within SLA, confirm filtering severity to critical and high returns zero rows for six consecutive runs.

Runbook linkage: document planned dataset migrations and schema evolution windows so validators do not file defects during stakeholder-approved population shifts.

### Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — PSI on the top revenue-driving feature crosses 0.25 after an upstream warehouse schema change: freeze automatic scoring, rebuild PSI bins from the last known good training snapshot, validate feature-store contracts, and open a prioritized retrain under change control.

Case 2 — A categorical feature gains a new high-cardinality level after a product launch: expect dataset cardinality explosion in training summaries; cap rare levels intentionally, refresh cardinality monitors, and widen container memory requests before retrain jobs repeat out-of-memory failures.

Case 3 — Class imbalance flips after a fraud policy change: accuracy may look flat while F1 collapses; add stratified metrics, review threshold policies, and confirm analysts agree on the new prevalence assumptions before accepting alerts as noise.

Case 4 — Accuracy regresses about twelve percent after a market regime change: promote shadow only if leakage audits pass, otherwise retrain with regime-aware features and document baseline resets for risk committees.

Case 5 — Retrain job out-of-memory on a larger dataset: shard training history, move to batch exports on indexers, pin Python scientific package versions, and validate DSDL container limits on Kubernetes workers.

Case 6 — Suspected data leak from feature engineering inflates shadow minus active accuracy: halt promotion, review temporal joins, and replay holdout evaluations with time-based splits only.

Case 7 — Labels arrive fourteen days late breaking a seven-day feedback window: fix ingest paths for outcomes, tighten service level agreements with business owners, and downgrade severity only when delay is an accepted operational reality documented in the runbook.

Case 8 — Shadow model outperforms active for two continuous weeks: run independent validation, confirm traffic routing fairness, and schedule promotion or demotion decisions with executive sign-off.

Case 9 — Feature store join key rename breaks lookups: expect sudden cold features and stability illusions; restore contract tests, revert schema releases, and rebuild training views before trusting drift math again.

Case 10 — Experiment cohort A/B mid-flight changes traffic ratio: expect prediction drift unrelated to model quality; tag experiment metadata on events and gate alerts with experiment identifiers.

Case 11 — Runtime apply errors spike after a Machine Learning Toolkit upgrade: compare extension versions, replay fit and apply on a canary search head, and roll back incompatible algorithm options until compatibility matrices are updated.

Case 12 — Scheduled search backlog causes prediction lag that looks like staleness: separate pipeline latency from model age by correlating _internal scheduler metrics and apply queue depth, then widen resources or split searches before retraining a healthy model.

## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-13.4.15 MLTK and DSDL model drift performance decay retrain trigger fusion mltk_model_metrics REST models mlspl dsdl sidecar feedback labels earliest=-24h@h")`
| multisearch
    [ | inputlookup mltk_model_metrics.csv
      | eval lane="mltk_lookup_metrics"
      | eval model_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model_name, model_id, "unknown_model"))))
      | eval env=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(env, environment, scoring_env, "prod"))))
      | eval alg=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(alg, algorithm, "mltk_ml_spl"))))
      | eval psi_score=tonumber(coalesce(psi_top_feature, psi_score, 0))
      | eval ks_stat=tonumber(coalesce(ks_top_feature, ks_stat, 0))
      | eval accuracy_7d=tonumber(coalesce(accuracy_rolling_7d, accuracy_7d, 0))
      | eval accuracy_baseline=tonumber(coalesce(accuracy_champion_baseline, accuracy_baseline, 0))
      | eval prediction_p95_ms=tonumber(coalesce(apply_latency_p95_ms, prediction_p95_ms, 0))
      | eval last_trained_epoch=tonumber(coalesce(last_fit_epoch, 0))
      | eval last_trained_age_days=if(last_trained_epoch>0, round((now()-last_trained_epoch)/86400,2), tonumber(coalesce(last_trained_age_days, 0)))
      | eval retrain_failure_count=tonumber(coalesce(retrain_fail_7d, retrain_failure_count, 0))
      | eval label_lag_days=tonumber(coalesce(label_feedback_lag_days, label_lag_days, 0))
      | eval shadow_acc=tonumber(coalesce(shadow_accuracy_7d, 0))
      | eval active_acc=tonumber(coalesce(active_accuracy_7d, accuracy_7d, 0))
      | eval shadow_minus_active_acc=round(shadow_acc-active_acc,4)
      | fields _time lane model_name env alg psi_score ks_stat accuracy_7d accuracy_baseline prediction_p95_ms last_trained_age_days retrain_failure_count label_lag_days shadow_minus_active_acc ]
    [ | rest /services/data/models splunk_server=local count=0
      | eval lane="rest_data_models"
      | eval model_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(title, "unknown_model"))))
      | eval env=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(app, "prod"))))
      | eval alg="mltk_persisted_model"
      | eval psi_score=0
      | eval ks_stat=0
      | eval accuracy_7d=0
      | eval accuracy_baseline=0
      | eval prediction_p95_ms=0
      | eval last_trained_age_days=if(isnotnull(updated) AND len(toString(updated))>=10, round((now()-strptime(substr(toString(updated),1,19), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"))/86400,2), 0)
      | eval retrain_failure_count=0
      | eval label_lag_days=0
      | eval shadow_minus_active_acc=0
      | fields _time lane model_name env alg psi_score ks_stat accuracy_7d accuracy_baseline prediction_p95_ms last_trained_age_days retrain_failure_count label_lag_days shadow_minus_active_acc ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd source=*mlspl.log earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval lane="internal_mlspl_apply_fit"
      | rex field=_raw max_match=1 "(?i)model[^=]*=\s*(?<ml_name>[A-Za-z0-9_.-]+)"
      | eval model_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(ml_name, "unknown_model"))))
      | eval env="prod"
      | eval alg="mltk_ml_spl"
      | eval psi_score=0
      | eval ks_stat=0
      | eval accuracy_7d=0
      | eval accuracy_baseline=0
      | eval prediction_p95_ms=tonumber(coalesce(apply_duration_ms, exec_duration_ms, 0))
      | eval last_trained_age_days=0
      | eval retrain_failure_count=if(match(lower(_raw),"error|exception|failed|oom|killed"),1,0)
      | eval label_lag_days=0
      | eval shadow_minus_active_acc=0
      | bucket _time span=15m
      | stats max(_time) AS _time perc95(prediction_p95_ms) AS prediction_p95_ms sum(retrain_failure_count) AS retrain_failure_count BY model_name env alg lane ]
    [ search index=dsdl_inference sourcetype=dsdl:metrics earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval lane="dsdl_container_metrics"
      | eval model_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(dsdl_model, model_name, container_model, "unknown_model"))))
      | eval env=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_namespace, env, "prod"))))
      | eval alg=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(framework, "pytorch_tf"))))
      | eval psi_score=tonumber(coalesce(prediction_psi, feature_psi, 0))
      | eval ks_stat=tonumber(coalesce(prediction_ks, 0))
      | eval accuracy_7d=tonumber(coalesce(batch_accuracy_7d, 0))
      | eval accuracy_baseline=tonumber(coalesce(batch_accuracy_baseline, 0))
      | eval prediction_p95_ms=tonumber(coalesce(inference_p95_ms, scoring_latency_ms, 0))
      | eval last_trained_age_days=tonumber(coalesce(model_age_days, 0))
      | eval retrain_failure_count=tonumber(coalesce(train_job_fail_count, 0))
      | eval label_lag_days=tonumber(coalesce(label_delay_days, 0))
      | eval shadow_minus_active_acc=tonumber(coalesce(shadow_gap_acc, 0))
      | fields _time lane model_name env alg psi_score ks_stat accuracy_7d accuracy_baseline prediction_p95_ms last_trained_age_days retrain_failure_count label_lag_days shadow_minus_active_acc ]
    [ search index=ml_feedback sourcetype=feedback:label earliest=-30d@d latest=now
      | eval lane="label_feedback_gap"
      | eval model_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model_name, predictor_id, "unknown_model"))))
      | eval env=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(env, "prod"))))
      | eval alg="labeled_feedback"
      | eval psi_score=0
      | eval ks_stat=0
      | eval accuracy_7d=0
      | eval accuracy_baseline=0
      | eval prediction_p95_ms=0
      | eval last_trained_age_days=0
      | eval retrain_failure_count=0
      | eval label_lag_days=tonumber(coalesce(label_gap_days, feedback_lag_days, 0))
      | eval shadow_minus_active_acc=0
      | stats max(_time) AS _time max(label_lag_days) AS label_lag_days BY model_name env alg lane ]
| eval model_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model_name, "unknown_model"))))
| eval env=coalesce(env, "prod")
| eval alg=coalesce(alg, "unknown_alg")
| bucket _time span=1h
| stats values(lane) AS lanes max(psi_score) AS psi_score max(ks_stat) AS ks_stat max(accuracy_7d) AS accuracy_7d max(accuracy_baseline) AS accuracy_baseline perc95(prediction_p95_ms) AS prediction_p95_ms max(last_trained_age_days) AS last_trained_age_days sum(retrain_failure_count) AS retrain_failure_count max(label_lag_days) AS label_lag_days max(shadow_minus_active_acc) AS shadow_minus_active_acc BY _time model_name env alg
| sort model_name env + _time
| streamstats window=168 global=f current=t avg(accuracy_7d) AS accuracy_7d_roll BY model_name env
| eval accuracy_7d=if(accuracy_7d>0, accuracy_7d, accuracy_7d_roll)
| eventstats median(accuracy_7d) AS accuracy_med_global BY model_name
| eval accuracy_baseline=if(accuracy_baseline>0, accuracy_baseline, coalesce(accuracy_med_global, accuracy_7d, 0))
| eval accuracy_drift_pct=if(accuracy_baseline>0.0001, round(100*(accuracy_baseline-accuracy_7d)/accuracy_baseline,2), 0)
| eventstats max(accuracy_baseline) AS acc_base_max BY model_name
| eval accuracy_baseline=coalesce(acc_base_max, accuracy_baseline)
| join type=left max=0 model_name
    [| inputlookup mltk_model_inventory.csv
     | eval model_name=lower(trim(toString(model_name)))
     | fields model_name owner_team business_use_case retrain_cadence_days sla_accuracy runbook_url ]
| join type=left max=0 model_name
    [| search index=cmdb sourcetype=ml_model_inventory earliest=-7d@h latest=now
     | dedup model_name
     | eval model_name=lower(trim(toString(model_name)))
     | fields model_name cmdb_owner_team cmdb_criticality]
| eval owner_team=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(owner_team, cmdb_owner_team, "unowned"))))
| eval business_use_case=coalesce(business_use_case, "unspecified_outcome")
| eval severity=case(psi_score>0.25 OR accuracy_drift_pct>10 OR last_trained_age_days>90 OR retrain_failure_count>=2, "critical", psi_score>0.20 OR accuracy_drift_pct>7 OR last_trained_age_days>60 OR label_lag_days>7 OR shadow_minus_active_acc>0.05, "high", psi_score>0.10 OR accuracy_drift_pct>4 OR prediction_p95_ms>5000 OR retrain_failure_count>=1, "medium", true(), "low")
| eval recommended_action=case(severity=="critical" AND psi_score>0.25, "freeze_scoring_rebucket_psi_validate_feature_contracts_and_schema", severity=="critical" AND accuracy_drift_pct>10, "rollback_model_promotion_retrain_with_fresh_labels_and_regime_features", severity=="critical" AND last_trained_age_days>90, "schedule_forced_retrain_verify_python_pips_and_mltk_dsdl_versions", severity=="critical" AND retrain_failure_count>=2, "repair_retrain_pipeline_check_memory_limits_and_dataset_cardinality", severity=="high" AND label_lag_days>7, "fix_label_feedback_ingest_tighten_ground_truth_join_sla", severity=="high" AND shadow_minus_active_acc>0.05, "open_shadow_promotion_review_independent_leakage_audit", severity=="high", "deep_dive_feature_store_joins_and_calibration_curves", severity=="medium", "tighten_monitoring_buckets_refresh_baseline_snapshot", true(), "continue_sampling_drift_metrics_and_champion_challenger_evidence")
| table _time model_name env alg owner_team business_use_case psi_score ks_stat accuracy_7d accuracy_drift_pct prediction_p95_ms last_trained_age_days retrain_failure_count label_lag_days severity recommended_action
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Performance.cpu_pct) AS cpu_pct latest(Performance.mem_used) AS mem_used FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
| rename Performance.host AS train_host
| join type=left max=0 train_host [| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Inventory.os) AS os latest(Inventory.cpu_count) AS cpu_count FROM datamodel=Inventory WHERE nodename=Inventory earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Inventory.dest | rename Inventory.dest AS train_host]
| where like(train_host,"%sh%") OR like(train_host,"%dsdl%") OR like(train_host,"%ml%")
| table train_host cpu_pct mem_used os cpu_count
```

## Visualization

Severity-colored table by model_name and env with drilldowns to mltk metrics rows, mlspl diagnostics, dsdl sidecar series, label feedback timelines, and lookup-enriched owner_team; dual-axis chart of accuracy_7d versus accuracy_drift_pct; single-value tiles for psi_score and last_trained_age_days.

## Known False Positives

Planned quarterly retrain windows routinely elevate last_trained_age_days until the new champion lands; require correlation with change calendars before paging executives. A/B holdout cohorts engineered to see harder cases can show lower active accuracy by design; join experiment metadata before treating drift as production failure. Holiday seasonality and fiscal close cycles shift legitimate priors; annotate known seasonal regimes so severity downgrades follow policy rather than ad hoc muting. Marketing campaigns may induce expected population shifts stakeholders already approved; treat alerts as confirmation, not surprises, when campaign tags are present. Dataset migrations and backfills can temporarily inflate cardinality and PSI until baselines refresh; gate alerts using migration tickets and post-migration stabilization hours. Shadow models that score a biased slice of traffic can look artificially strong; validate cohort parity before promotion narratives. Short outages in label ingest mimic long label_lag_days until queues catch up; verify pipeline lag separately from model quality. REST snapshot delays after upgrades can inflate staleness metrics until the first successful poll; compare against bundle versions. Canary search heads running experimental Python pins may emit divergent accuracy telemetry; segregate environments in inventory. Internal penetration tests that replay historical features can spike KS statistics without threatening production; correlate with security calendars.

- [arXiv — Learning under concept drift: a review](https://arxiv.org/abs/2004.05785)
